images.caption: Figure 1:Iterative message exchange among agents enables progressive refinement of predicted action distributions over multiple communication rounds.

Figure 2:Overview of the proposed LC-MAPF architecture.
Each agentu∈𝒰u\in\mathcal{U}encodes its local observationouto_{u}^{t}into a latent representationzuz_{u}using a Transformer-based encoder. The latent state participates in an iterative message-passing procedure overRRcommunication rounds. At each roundrr, the agent receives the set of neighbor messagesCurC_{u}^{r}and fuses them with its latent statezuz_{u}through a Transformer-based decoder, producing an updated messagemurm_{u}^{r}that will be sent to its neighbors in the next round. AfterRRcommunication rounds, the decoder outputs action logitsaua_{u}, which are converted to an action probability distributionpu=softmax​(au)p_{u}=\mathrm{softmax}(a_{u}). Both encoder and decoder consist of stacked Transformer blocks with self-attention and cross-attention: the encoder integrates the tokenized observationouto_{u}^{t}, while the decoder integrates the agent’s latent representationzuz_{u}with contextualized neighbor messagesCurC_{u}^{r}, enabling decentralized coordination through end-to-end learned communication.
Figure 3:Success rates of the approaches on different map types depending on the number of agents in the instances (higher is better). The shaded area indicates the 95% confidence interval.
Figure 4:SoC ratio relative to solutions found by the LaCAM* approach (lower is better).
Figure 5:All agents and the corresponding actions that may result in a collision with the reference agent (marked as green).
Figure 6:Success rate of LC-MAPF and the evaluated baselines with collision shielding enabled.
Figure 7:Relative solution cost (SoC) of LC-MAPF and the evaluated baselines with collision shielding enabled.
Figure 8:Left: Modular and reconfigurable maze environment composed of standardized floor and wall units. Right: Deeply modified robotic agent based on Jetbot AI Kit.
Figure 9:Real-world execution of a maze scenario with 3 robots. Left: simulated environment in POGEMA. Right: corresponding physical setup used for real-world deployment.
